questions.question:
Let the function
\(f(\theta)=\begin{vmatrix} sin\theta & cos\theta & tan\theta \\ sin(\frac{\pi}{6}) & cos(\frac{\pi}{6}) & tan(\frac{\pi}{6})\\ sin(\frac{\pi}{3})& cos(\frac{\pi}{3}) & tan(\frac{\pi}{3}) \end{vmatrix}\)
where \(\theta \in [\frac{\pi}{6},\frac{\pi}{3}]\) and \(f'(\theta )\) denote the derivative of f with respect to \(\theta\) . Which of the following statements is/are TRUE?
(I) There existrs \(\theta \in (\frac{\pi}{6},\frac{\pi}{3})\) such that \(f'(\theta )=0\)
(I) There existrs \(\theta \in (\frac{\pi}{6},\frac{\pi}{3})\) such that \(f'(\theta )\neq 0\)

(GATE CSE 2014 SET-1)
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Which one of the following functions is continuous at x = 3?

(GATE CSE 2013)
options:
A.

\(f(x)=\left\{\begin{matrix} 2, & if \; x=3\\ x-1,& if \; x \gt 3\\ \frac{x+3}{3},&if \; x \lt 3 \end{matrix}\right.\)
questions.correct_answer:
A.

\(f(x)=\left\{\begin{matrix} 2, & if \; x=3\\ x-1,& if \; x \gt 3\\ \frac{x+3}{3},&if \; x \lt 3 \end{matrix}\right.\)
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Consider the set of (column) vectors defined by
\(X=\{x\in R^{3}|x_{1}+x_{2}+x_{3}=0, \; where \; x^{T}=[x_{1},x_{2},x_{3}]^{T}\}.\)
Which of the following is TRUE?

(GATE CSE 2007)
options:
A.

\(\{[1,-1,0]^{T},[1,0,-1]^{T}\}\) is a basis for the subspace X.

B.

\(\{[1,-1,0]^{T},[1,0,-1]^{T}\}\) is a linearly independent set, but it does not span X and therefore is not a basis of X.
questions.correct_answer:
A.

\(\{[1,-1,0]^{T},[1,0,-1]^{T}\}\) is a basis for the subspace X.
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Consider the following two statements about the function f(x)=|x|:

P. f(x) is continuous for all real values of x
Q. f(x) is differentiable for all real values of x

Which of the following is TRUE?

(GATE CSE 2007)
options:
A.

P is true and Q is false.

B.

P is false and Q is true.
questions.correct_answer:
A.

P is true and Q is false.
